description:
Goal: make compiler2 native codegen compiler2-owned end to end, so the compiler2 path no longer reprojects its facts into old planner/codegen shapes or a second type world before JIT/AOT. Signal: compiler2 JIT/AOT/runtime entry points consume a compiler2-owned native backend path whose semantic inputs are `NativeProgram(root)` plus the one world-owned compiler2 `Types`; compiler2 no longer depends on old `ir_codegen` surfaces that require `SpecPlan`, `SpecRegistry`, `crate::types::Ty`, or legacy dispatch/type-test reprojections.

Grounding from the current code:
- compiler2 already closes its backend handoff as `NativeProgram` in `src/compiler2/artifact.rs`, with bodies, ABI lanes, value types, callable-entry inventory, closure-call targets, extern marshals, and effects.
- compiler2 still calls `ir_codegen::compile_with_backend_native_program` from `src/compiler2/compiler.rs`, seeding it with a fresh `crate::types::new()` and therefore a second type owner.
- the shared native-codegen path still rebuilds planner-shaped scaffolding for compiler2 in `src/ir_codegen/driver.rs`: synthetic `SpecKey`, fake `SpecPlan`, empty `SpecRegistry`, and `return_tys` widened to `any`.
- compiler2 native lowering still translates compiler2 facts back into old-world payloads in `src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs`: legacy extern contracts, legacy dispatch/guard structures, and `legacy_type_test_ty`.
- some backend/runtime mechanics are genuinely universal, but the code that reasons about types, dispatch, callable selection, extern contracts, helper ownership, and body identity is only shared on paper; in practice it is pinned to old-world inputs.

Strategy:
- publish the exact in-house compiler2 native-codegen contract first, including the ownership cut between compiler2-native lowering and truly universal backend/runtime mechanics.
- copy the native-codegen logic that depends on compiler2 facts into compiler2 and retarget it directly onto `NativeProgram`, compiler2 `Ty`, and compiler2-owned dispatch/type-test descriptors.
- change the compiler2 native handoff/data model where necessary so the backend consumes the real facts directly instead of rebuilding old planner/codegen state under new names.
- switch compiler2 front doors onto the in-house backend, delete the old-world dead weight from the compiler2 route, then close with docs/telemetry and restate the remaining migration problem if one still exists.

What should go away:
- compiler2 calls to `ir_codegen::compile_with_backend_native_program` and the fresh `crate::types::new()` in `src/compiler2/compiler.rs`.
- compiler2-native use of fake `SpecPlan`, fake `SpecRegistry`, synthetic `SpecKey`, and widened `return_tys` in the native backend route.
- compiler2-native re-projection of extern contracts, dispatch facts, or type tests into old `crate::types::Ty` / legacy dispatch payloads.
- any compiler2-native dependency on old planner/type-preparation surfaces after `NativeProgram(root)`.

Checks:
- compiler2 quicksort, `Enum.reduce`, spawn/receive, variadic extern, and tail-recursion fixtures still run through JIT/AOT with identical behavior.
- compiler2 telemetry still proves there is no fallback into planner/type-infer work, and new backend telemetry makes the native path observable at its real seam.
- docs describe the ownership cut precisely enough that returning to `fz-rh2.9` is either tractable or obviously unnecessary.
title: Compiler2 native backends: bring native codegen in-house and delete old-world backend dead weight
